subject: Biology
title: The Evolution of Aging
description: A fast breakdown of The Evolution of Aging for builders.

hook.text: Greenland sharks live over 400 years. Mayflies live one day. Why does lifespan vary so dramatically?

buildup.text: Natural selection is strongest in young, reproductive individuals and weakens with age.

discovery.text: Harmful genes that act late in life aren't removed by selection—nobody reproduced that late to be affected.

twist.text: Some organisms don't age. Hydra and certain jellyfish show negligible senescence—they can theoretically live forever.

climax.text: Understanding why aging evolved could lead to interventions that extend healthy human lifespan.

punchline.text: Aging isn't a design flaw. It's a priority trade-off.

quiz.question: Why do harmful late-acting genes persist in populations?
options: Natural selection is too weak at old ages to remove them
They are beneficial early in life only
They mutated too recently to be eliminated
